compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I receive a value-based priority instead of default P2 when using brainstorm or roadmap
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Create a shared standalone value/effort scorer that maps epics to recommended priorities (P0-P4) and integrate it into both /brainstorm and /roadmap
**Why**: Brainstorm assigns default P2 to all epics. Roadmap has an inline value/effort matrix but it is not shared. A standalone scorer enables both skills to assign meaningful priorities
**Scope**:
- IN: Create shared scorer workflow with documented priority matrix; integrate into /brainstorm (replace default P2); integrate into /roadmap (replace inline matrix)
- OUT: Reprioritizing existing backlog; changing priority semantics beyond P0-P4

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a shared value/effort scorer exists with a documented matrix mapping value x effort to P0-P4
  ← Satisfies: "shared standalone value/effort scorer accepts a structured epic description"
- When this story is complete, /brainstorm uses the scorer to assign a non-default priority and /roadmap uses the scorer in place of its inline matrix
  ← Satisfies: "/brainstorm uses it to replace the default P2 assignment; /roadmap uses it in place of its current inline matrix"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Compatibility] Roadmap currently uses a 1-10 scale for Value and Effort. The scorer must decide on a scale (1-5 or 1-10) and update the appropriate skill's instructions to match. If 1-5, roadmap's Phase 3 matrix presentation must be rewritten. If 1-10, the brainstorm integration must use the same scale
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: shared value/effort scorer created and integrated into both brainstorm and roadmap
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
